label: 123_transparency cbo_agencys_cost estimates_cbos
description: The Congressional Budget Office is enhancing its transparency by publishing new modeling tools, source code, and technical documentation. These actions aim to promote understanding of CBO’s analyses and engage the public in its work.

earliest_articles.title: CBO Joins Twitter
earliest_articles.excerpt: September 9, 2013 CBO is excited to launch its social media presence on Twitter. You will be able to follow us on Twitter in two ways, depending on your interests: - Our main account—@USCBO—will announce our reports and selected cost estimates. - Our second account—@USCBOCostEst—will announce all of our cost…

earliest_articles.title: CBO Launches YouTube Channel
earliest_articles.excerpt: September 12, 2014 CBO is excited to announce the launch of its YouTube channel! We will post videos of Congressional testimonies, press briefings, and other events involving CBO at www.youtube.com/uscbo. CBO is proud of its success in providing nonpartisan and insightful analyses to the Congress, and we consider the…

newest_articles.title: Transparency at CBO: Plans for 2026 and a Review of 2025
newest_articles.excerpt: Transparency at CBO: Plans for 2026 and a Review of 2025 Report In 2026, CBO will promote transparency by publishing new modeling tools, source code, and technical documentation and by explaining the methods it uses for its analyses in several topic areas. Transparency is a top priority for the Congressional Budget…

newest_articles.title: CBO’s Recent Publications and Work in Progress as of March 31, 2026
newest_articles.excerpt: Analytical Reports In addition to cost estimates, CBO has released 29 reports, testimonies, and other analytical products since publishing its last quarterly report on the agency’s work in progress (see Table 1). Notably, CBO continued to bolster its efforts to be transparent about its methods and data. As part of…

newest_articles.title: CBO Expands Modeling Resources on GitHub
newest_articles.excerpt: In response to Congressional interest and as part of our ongoing efforts to enhance transparency, the Congressional Budget Office plans to publish additional computer code on our GitHub page throughout the year showing details of the modeling underlying CBO's analyses. Last week, we published several new code…

central_articles.title: Transparency at CBO: Future Plans and a Review of 2021
central_articles.excerpt: At a Glance The Congressional Budget Office’s transparency efforts are intended to promote a thorough understanding of its work, help people gauge how estimates might change if policies or circumstances differed, and enhance the credibility of its analyses and processes.
